nameMeanings.name: Perrin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Perrin, a pet form of Old French Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perine , Perrine , and Prine .

History: Henri Perrin from Louargat in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Jeanne Merrin in Montreal, QC, in 1661. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variants or altered forms Perrine and Pareyn) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the Huguenot ancestors was Daniel Perrin (1642–1719), nicknamed The Huguenot, who arrived in New York in 1665, aboard the Philip, and settled first in NJ and later on Staten Island. Among his descendants the surname is common in the altered forms Perrine, Perine and Prine. The Staten Island community of Huguenot is named after Daniel Perrin and other Huguenots who settled in the area during the late 17th century.

variants.name: Prine
meanings: Americanized form of French   Perrin  .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Dutch   Pruyn   or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.
History: The surname Prine is established among the descendants of the Huguenot immigrant Daniel Perrin (1642–1719); see Perrin .

variants.name: Pierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Antoine, Yves, Michel, Jean Claude, Mireille, Jean Robert, Francois, Monique, Raoul, Serge.
French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian:

from the personal name Pierre, French form of Peter (see also below). This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Jean and Joseph ) and in Saint Martin. It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in Trinidad and Tobago, Saint Lucia, Grenada, and Bahamas).

variants.name: Perren
meanings: French and English (of French Huguenot origin): variant of   Perrin  .

variants.name: Parren
meanings: English: variant of   Perrin  .

variants.name: Perrine
meanings: French: from the feminine form of   Perrin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
English: variant of   Perrin  . This surname is now rare in Britain and Ireland.

History: The surname Perrine of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with its original form Perrin in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Parran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
Altered form of French   Perrin  . This surname was brought to England by the Huguenots, but has died out there.
In some cases possibly also southern French: topographic name from Occitan parran ‘small garden behind a house’. This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Perrins
meanings: English (West Midlands): variant of Perrin , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Selwoode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Selwood in Somerset, close to the border with Wilts, or from Selwood (Forest), from which the village was named. The forest lies in Somerset, Wilts, and Dorset.
2  English: reduced form of   Silverwood  .

variants.name: Sellwood
meanings: English:  see  Selwood  .

variants.name: Sillwood
meanings: English:  see  Selwood  .

variants.name: Silwood
meanings: 1  English:  see  Selwood  .
2  English: reduced form of   Silverwood  .

variants.name: Silverwood
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more places called with Middle English silver ‘silver’ (Old English seolfor) + wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu), presumably referring to the color of birch trees. One source of the surname appears to be a lost place in Greasley (Nottinghamshire), on the edge of Sherwood Forest. Another source may be Silver Wood in Ravenfield (Yorkshire), and there may have been yet another in the Craven area of Yorkshire. The surname was sometimes shortened to Silwood and Selwood.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Selwoode Perrin was born in 1503, in Chard, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Perrin. She died in 1581, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Selwoode Perrin est née en 1503, à Chard, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Thomas Perrin. Elle est décédée en 1581, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mary Selwoode Perrin wurde 1503 in Chard, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Thomas Perrin mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1581 im Alter von 78 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Mary Selwoode Perrin è nata il nel 1503 nel Chard, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Thomas Perrin. Morì nel 1581, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 78 anni.
